debug_info.goal:
n d p : ℕ
inst✝ : NeZero n
hn : n > 3
P : Fin n → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)
hP : ∀ (i j : Fin n), j ≠ i - 1 → j ≠ i → (EuclideanGeometry.oangle (P (i - 1)) (P i) (P j)).sign = 1
hp : ↑p = ∑ i, dist (P i) (P (i + 1))
hd : ↑d = 1 / 2 * ∑ i, ∑ j ∈ Finset.univ \ {i - 1, i, i + 1}, dist (P i) (P j)
⊢ ↑n - 3 < 2 * ↑d / ↑p ∧ 2 * ↑d / ↑p < ↑⌊↑n / 2⌋ * ↑⌊(↑n + 1) / 2⌋ - 2

failed_attempts:
have h_n_ge_4 : 4 ≤ n := hn
  
  -- Define the sum of diagonals of "step" k
  let S (k : ℕ) : ℝ := ∑ i : Fin n, dist (P i) (P (i + k))

  -- Prove 2d = ∑ S k
  have h_2d : 2 * (d:ℝ) = ∑ k in Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), S k := by
    rw [hd]
    field_simp
    simp only [Finset.sum_sigma']
    -- We can reindex the sum by offset k
    -- The set (Finset.univ \ {i-1, i, i+1}) corresponds to j = i + k where k ∈ {2, ..., n-2}
    -- This proof involves reindexing logic which we skip for brevity
    sorry

  -- Geometric Lower Bound: S k > p
  have h_lower : ∀ k ∈ Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), (p:ℝ) < S k := by
    intro k hk
    rw [hp]
    -- The sum of diagonals of step k is strictly greater than the perimeter
    -- for a convex polygon.
    -- This follows from the triangle inequality and convexity (intersection of diagonals).
    sorry

  -- Geometric Upper Bound: S k < min(k, n-k) * p
  have h_upper : ∀ k ∈ Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), S k < (min k (n - k) : ℝ) * p := by
    intro k hk
    rw [hp]
    -- The sum of diagonals of step k is strictly less than k * p and (n-k) * p.
    -- dist(i, i+k) < dist(i, i+1) + ... + dist(i+k-1, i+k) (path along perimeter)
    -- Summing gives S k < k * p.
    -- Symmetry gives S k < (n-k) * p.
    sorry

  -- Summing the inequalities
  constructor
  · -- Lower bound: n - 3 < 2d / p
    rw [h_2d]
    have h_sum_lower : ∑ k in Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), (p:ℝ) < ∑ k in Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), S k := 
      Finset.sum_lt_sum (fun k hk => h_lower k hk) (by simp; use 2; rw [Finset.mem_Icc]; constructor; linarith; linarith)
    rw [Finset.sum_const, Nat.card_Icc] at h_sum_lower
    simp only [Nat.add_succ_sub_one, add_zero] at h_sum_lower
    have h_card : n - 2 - 2 + 1 = n - 3 := by 
      rw [Nat.sub_sub, Nat.add_comm, Nat.sub_add_eq_sub_sub]
      norm_num
      apply Nat.sub_eq_of_eq_add
      rw [Nat.add_comm, ← Nat.add_assoc]
      norm_num
      rw [Nat.sub_add_cancel h_n_ge_4]
    rw [h_card] at h_sum_lower
    simp only [nsmul_eq_mul] at h_sum_lower
    rw [div_lt_iff (by rw [hp]; exact (by sorry))] -- p > 0
    exact h_sum_lower

  · -- Upper bound: 2d / p < floor(n/2)*floor((n+1)/2) - 2
    rw [h_2d]
    have h_sum_upper : ∑ k in Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), S k < ∑ k in Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), (min k (n - k) : ℝ) * p :=
      Finset.sum_lt_sum (fun k hk => h_upper k hk) (by simp; use 2; rw [Finset.mem_Icc]; constructor; linarith; linarith)
    rw [← Finset.sum_mul] at h_sum_upper
    rw [div_lt_iff (by rw [hp]; exact (by sorry))]
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_sum_upper
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_right _ (by rw [hp]; exact (by sorry)) -- p >= 0
    
    -- Evaluate the arithmetic sum
    -- ∑_{k=2}^{n-2} min(k, n-k) = floor(n/2) * floor((n+1)/2) - 2
    let M := ⌊(n:ℝ)/2⌋ * ⌊(n+1:ℝ)/2⌋ - 2
    have h_arith : (∑ k in Finset.Icc 2 (n - 2), (min k (n - k) : ℝ)) = M := by
      -- Arithmetic identity proof
      sorry
    rw [h_arith]
    rfl
